schemeSetu.description: Comprehensive collection of Central Government and Punjab State Government schemes for age groups 6 to 45 years.

schemes.name: Samagra Shiksha Abhiyan
schemes.description: An integrated umbrella scheme for school education covering pre-primary to Class XII. It merges three earlier schemes — Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan, Rashtriya Madhyamik Shiksha Abhiyan, and Teacher Education — into one comprehensive program for quality and inclusive education.

eligibility.residency: All Indian students in government or government-aided schools
eligibility.otherCriteria: Special focus on marginalized groups, children with disabilities, and girls

financial: Free textbooks provided to students of Classes 1 to 8
Free uniforms for girls and SC/ST students in Classes 1 to 8
Transport allowance of up to Rs. 6,000/year for children in remote areas
Stipend of Rs. 200/month for girls with disabilities in Classes 9 to 12
nonFinancial: Infrastructure development in government schools
Digital classrooms and smart boards under ICT scheme
Sports equipment and library books in schools
Inclusive education support for children with special needs (CWSN)

howToApply: Step 1: Enroll the child in the nearest government or government-aided school
Step 2: Register with the school office with required documents
Step 3: Benefits like uniforms and textbooks are distributed automatically through the school
Step 4: For transport allowance, apply via the District Education Office
documentsRequired: Aadhaar card of child and parent

schemes.name: PM POSHAN Scheme (Mid-Day Meal)
schemes.description: One of the world's largest school feeding programs providing hot-cooked nutritious meals to students in government and government-aided schools daily to improve nutrition, boost school attendance, and reduce dropout rates.

eligibility.residency: Students enrolled in government or government-aided schools
eligibility.otherCriteria: Also covers Madrasas supported under Samagra Shiksha

financial: Free hot-cooked meal every school day
Meal cost: Rs. 6.19/day per child for primary; Rs. 9.29/day for upper primary (from Dec 2024)
nonFinancial: Improved nutrition and reduction in classroom hunger
Higher school attendance and retention rates
Reduction in malnutrition among school-going children

howToApply: Step 1: Enroll child in a government or government-aided school
Step 2: Meal is automatically provided on all school days — no separate application needed

schemes.name: Beti Bachao Beti Padhao (BBBP)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
schemes.description: A national campaign to address the declining Child Sex Ratio and promote the education and empowerment of the girl child by preventing female foeticide and improving enrollment and retention of girls in schools.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Special focus on districts with low child sex ratio

financial: Linked to Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana with 8.2% interest per annum
nonFinancial: Awareness campaigns against female foeticide and child marriage
Support for girl child school enrollment and retention
Conditional cash transfer through convergence with other schemes

howToApply: Step 1: Visit the nearest post office or authorized bank
Step 2: Open a Sukanya Samriddhi Account for the girl child
Step 3: Submit required documents
Step 4: Deposit minimum Rs. 250 to activate the account
documentsRequired: Birth certificate of the girl child

schemes.name: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY)
schemes.description: A government-backed small savings scheme for the girl child offering one of the highest interest rates among savings instruments. Part of the BBBP campaign, it encourages parents to save for their daughter's education and marriage.

eligibility.ageLimit: Below 10 years at time of account opening
eligibility.otherCriteria: Maximum 2 accounts per family; exception for twins/triplets

financial: Interest rate of 8.2% per annum (compounded annually)
Minimum deposit: Rs. 250/year; Maximum: Rs. 1,50,000/year
50% partial withdrawal allowed after girl turns 18
Complete tax exemption under Section 80C and on maturity amount
Matures when girl turns 21

howToApply: Step 1: Visit any post office or authorized bank (SBI, PNB, etc.)
Step 2: Fill SSY account opening form
Step 3: Submit documents and make initial deposit
Step 4: Collect passbook

schemes.name: National Means-cum-Merit Scholarship (NMMS)
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scholarship scheme to support meritorious students from low-income families to reduce dropout rates at Class 8 and encourage them to continue education at secondary level.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Must have scored 55% in Class 7 (50% for SC/ST); family income below Rs. 3.5 lakh/year; must be studying in government school

financial: Scholarship of Rs. 12,000 per year (Rs. 1,000/month) for Classes 9 to 12
Paid directly to student's bank account via DBT

howToApply: Step 1: Apply on the National Scholarship Portal at scholarships.gov.in
Step 2: Appear for the State-level NMMS Exam conducted by State Government
Step 3: Selected students receive scholarship automatically upon verification
Step 4: Renewal required each year by submitting progress report
documentsRequired: Income certificate of parent (below Rs. 3.5 lakh)

schemes.name: National Scheme of Incentive to Girls for Secondary Education (NSIGSE)
schemes.description: Provides financial incentive to SC/ST girl students who pass Class 8 to encourage enrollment in secondary school and reduce girl child dropouts.

eligibility.ageLimit: Below 16 years at time of enrollment in Class 9
eligibility.otherCriteria: SC/ST girls; passed Class 8; enrolled in Class 9 in a government school; unmarried

financial: Rs. 3,000 deposited in a fixed deposit account
Amount withdrawable with interest after turning 18 and passing Class 10

howToApply: Step 1: Apply through school principal after passing Class 8
Step 2: School submits eligible girls' list to District Education Officer
Step 3: Verified girls get a fixed deposit account opened in their name

schemes.name: Kasturba Gandhi Balika Vidyalaya (KGBV)
schemes.description: Residential schools set up at upper primary level for girls from marginalized communities in educationally backward blocks, ensuring quality education and safe stay for girls aged 10 to 18.

eligibility.otherCriteria: SC, ST, OBC, minority community girls; girls from BPL families; school dropout girls

nonFinancial: Free residential accommodation
Free meals (breakfast, lunch, dinner)
Free education from Class 6 to 12
Free uniforms, books, stationery
Vocational training and life skills education

howToApply: Step 1: Contact the nearest KGBV school or Block Education Office
Step 2: Submit application form with required documents
Step 3: Selection is done based on eligibility by the district authorities
Step 4: Admitted girls are provided all residential facilities free of cost
documentsRequired: Aadhaar card of student and parent

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Poshan Shakti Nirman (Mission Poshan 2.0)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
schemes.description: An integrated nutrition support program to address stunting, wasting, and anemia among children under 6 and adolescent girls, delivered through Anganwadi Centres using a technology-driven approach.

eligibility.ageLimit: Children 6 months to 6 years; Adolescent girls 14 to 18 years
eligibility.otherCriteria: Also covers pregnant and lactating mothers

nonFinancial: Supplementary nutrition — Take Home Ration or hot cooked meals
Early Childhood Care and Education (ECCE) for children 3 to 6 years
Iron, Vitamin B12, and Folic Acid supplements
Regular health check-ups and immunization
Nutrition tracking via Poshan Tracker app
Millet-based meals for better nutrition

howToApply: Step 1: Visit the nearest Anganwadi Centre
Step 2: Register the child or pregnant woman with the Anganwadi worker
Step 3: Provide required documents
Step 4: Benefits are provided automatically after enrollment
documentsRequired: MCP card (for infants and pregnant women)

schemes.name: PM CARES for Children
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
schemes.description: A comprehensive support scheme for children who lost both parents or their surviving guardian due to COVID-19, providing education, health insurance, and financial support until age 23.

eligibility.ageLimit: Up to 18 years at time of application (support until 23)
eligibility.otherCriteria: Both parents or surviving guardian died due to COVID-19 from March 11, 2020 onwards

financial: Corpus of Rs. 10 lakh built by age 18
Monthly stipend from age 18 to 23
Lump sum of Rs. 10 lakh at age 23
Rs. 4,000/month for children staying with relatives (Mission Vatsalya)
Rs. 20,000 school scholarship for Classes 1 to 12
Rs. 50,000/year Swanath Scholarship for technical education
nonFinancial: Health insurance of Rs. 5 lakh under Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY
Admission support in Kendriya/Navodaya Vidyalayas
Interest-free education loan for professional courses

howToApply: Step 1: Visit pmcaresforchildren.in
Step 2: Register as new applicant
Step 3: Upload COVID-19 death certificate of parent and child's documents
Step 4: District Magistrate verifies and approves
documentsRequired: Death certificate of parent mentioning COVID-19
Aadhaar card of child and guardian

schemes.name: Right to Education (RTE) Act – Free & Compulsory Education
schemes.description: A landmark law ensuring free and compulsory education to all children aged 6 to 14 years. It mandates that private schools reserve 25% of seats for children from weaker sections and disadvantaged groups.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Children from EWS (Economically Weaker Section) or disadvantaged groups for 25% private school quota

financial: Completely free education in government schools
Free education (including tuition fee) for EWS/disadvantaged children in private schools under 25% quota
nonFinancial: No detention policy till Class 8
Neighborhood school within 1 km for Classes 1 to 5
Trained teachers and proper infrastructure mandated

howToApply: Step 1: For government school — directly enroll child at the nearest government school
Step 2: For 25% private school quota — apply through District Education Office or state RTE portal
Step 3: Submit income certificate and address proof
Step 4: School admission is granted after verification
documentsRequired: Aadhaar card of child and parent

schemes.name: Pre-Matric Scholarship for SC/ST/OBC Students
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Social Justice / Tribal Affairs
schemes.description: Scholarship provided to SC, ST, and OBC students studying in Classes 9 and 10 to reduce the dropout rate and encourage continuation of education at the secondary level.

eligibility.otherCriteria: SC/ST/OBC category; family income below Rs. 2.5 lakh/year; enrolled in government or recognized school

financial: Day scholar: Rs. 225/month (Class 9-10)
Hosteller: Rs. 525/month (Class 9-10)
Additional allowances for books, clothing, and stationery

howToApply: Step 1: Apply on National Scholarship Portal at scholarships.gov.in
Step 2: Fill in personal, bank, and school details
Step 3: Upload required documents
Step 4: School verifies and forwards to district authority

schemes.name: Balika Samriddhi Yojana (BSY)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
schemes.description: Supports girl children from BPL families with a birth grant and annual school scholarships to improve their status and encourage education up to Class 10.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Maximum 2 girls per family; must be enrolled in school

financial: Rs. 500 cash grant at birth
Annual scholarships from Class 1 to 10 (Rs. 300 to Rs. 1,000/year based on class)

howToApply: Step 1: Visit nearest Anganwadi worker or ICDS office
Step 2: Fill application form for BSY
Step 3: Submit documents for verification
Step 4: Post office or bank account opened in girl's name; scholarship credited annually
documentsRequired: BPL ration card or income certificate

schemes.name: NPS Vatsalya Scheme
schemes.description: A new pension savings scheme introduced in Budget 2024 allowing parents and guardians to open and contribute to a National Pension System account for their minor children until they turn 18.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Managed by parent or legal guardian until child turns 18

financial: Long-term compounded growth of savings
Tax benefits similar to regular NPS
Seamless conversion to regular NPS account at age 18

howToApply: Step 1: Visit a PFRDA-registered Point of Presence (PoP) such as a bank or post office
Step 2: Fill the NPS Vatsalya account opening form
Step 3: Submit required documents
Step 4: Make initial contribution and receive PRAN for the minor

schemes.name: Bebe Nanki Laadli Beti Kalyan Scheme
schemes.ministry: Department of Social Security and Women & Child Development, Punjab
schemes.description: A Punjab-specific scheme to curb female foeticide and improve sex ratio by providing tiered financial assistance to girl children from birth to age 18 through a LIC-backed disbursement model.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Includes abandoned girls in Punjab orphanages born after Jan 1, 2011

financial: Rs. 20,000 deposited with LIC of India per beneficiary
Total payout of Rs. 61,000 disbursed at different life milestones up to age 18

howToApply: Step 1: Visit the nearest Anganwadi Centre or District Social Security Office in Punjab
Step 2: Fill the application form for this scheme
Step 3: Submit documents for verification
Step 4: Upon verification, amount is deposited with LIC in girl's name

schemes.name: Mai Bhago Vidya Scheme (Free Bicycle)
schemes.ministry: Department of Social Security and Women & Child Development, Punjab
schemes.description: Encourages girl students in Classes 9 to 12 in Punjab government schools to continue education by providing them free bicycles, reducing commute barriers and school dropouts.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Enrolled in government school in Punjab

nonFinancial: Free bicycle provided to each eligible girl student
Encourages enrollment and reduces dropout in secondary education

howToApply: Step 1: Apply through the school where the girl is enrolled
Step 2: School prepares and submits eligible girls list to District Education Office
Step 3: Bicycles are distributed at the school level

schemes.name: Punjab Ashirwad (Shagun) Scheme
schemes.ministry: Department of Social Security and Women & Child Development, Punjab
schemes.description: Provides financial assistance of Rs. 51,000 to families from economically weaker sections for the marriage of their girl child, to reduce the financial burden of marriage and prevent child marriage.

eligibility.ageLimit: Girl must be 18 years or above at time of marriage
eligibility.otherCriteria: BPL families; SC/ST/OBC; widows/divorced women applying for daughters; orphan girls

financial: Rs. 51,000 financial assistance per girl for marriage

howToApply: Step 1: Visit the District Social Security Office in Punjab
Step 2: Fill the Ashirwad Scheme application form
Step 3: Submit documents before marriage date
Step 4: After verification, amount is deposited directly in applicant's account
documentsRequired: Aadhaar card of girl and parents
Marriage invitation card or proof of marriage date

schemes.name: Scholarship for Students with Disabilities (Punjab)
schemes.ministry: Department of Social Security and Women & Child Development, Punjab
schemes.description: A 100% state-funded monthly scholarship for students with disabilities studying in schools in Punjab to support their education and enable them to earn a livelihood.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Must have recognized disability certificate; enrolled in a school in Punjab

financial: Rs. 200/month for students in Classes 1 to 8
Rs. 300/month for students in Class 9 and above

howToApply: Step 1: Obtain disability certificate from Chief Medical Officer (CMO)
Step 2: Visit District Social Security Office in Punjab
Step 3: Fill application form and submit documents
Step 4: Monthly scholarship credited to bank account after verification
documentsRequired: Aadhaar card of student and parent

schemes.name: PM YASASVI Scholarship Scheme (OBC/EBC/DNT)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.description: PM Young Achievers Scholarship Award Scheme for Vibrant India — provides scholarships to students from OBC, EBC, and DNT communities studying in Classes 9 and 11 to support their secondary and senior secondary education.

eligibility.ageLimit: Class 9 students (born between 2004-2009); Class 11 students (born between 2002-2007)
eligibility.otherCriteria: OBC/EBC/DNT community; family income below Rs. 2.5 lakh/year; must qualify the YASASVI entrance test

financial: Rs. 75,000/year for students in Classes 9 and 10
Rs. 1,25,000/year for students in Classes 11 and 12

howToApply: Step 1: Apply on the National Scholarship Portal at scholarships.gov.in
Step 2: Appear for the YASASVI Entrance Test (YET) conducted by NTA
Step 3: Selected students are notified and scholarship is credited via DBT

schemes.name: AICTE Swanath Scholarship
schemes.description: Provides financial support to orphan students, children of armed forces martyrs, and COVID-19 orphans pursuing diploma or undergraduate technical education in AICTE-approved institutions.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Orphan students; wards of martyred armed forces/paramilitary personnel; COVID-19 orphans; family income below Rs. 8 lakh/year

financial: Rs. 50,000 per year scholarship for the duration of the course

howToApply: Step 1: Apply through the AICTE portal at aicte-india.org
Step 2: Fill the Swanath Scholarship form with all details
Step 3: Upload supporting documents
Step 4: Institution verifies and AICTE releases the scholarship
documentsRequired: Proof of orphan status / death certificate of parent (armed forces/COVID-19)

schemes.name: Punjab Chief Minister's Scholarship Scheme
schemes.description: Provides financial assistance to meritorious students from economically weaker sections in Punjab studying in government schools and colleges, to ensure that lack of money does not become a barrier to quality education.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Meritorious students from EWS families; studying in government institutions in Punjab

financial: Tuition fee waiver for eligible students
Monthly stipend for living expenses (amount varies by level of education)

howToApply: Step 1: Apply through the Punjab scholarship portal at scholarships.punjab.gov.in
Step 2: Fill in all personal and academic details
Step 3: Upload required documents
Step 4: School or college verifies and forwards the application

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY 4.0)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
schemes.description: India's flagship skill training scheme that provides free short-term skill training to Indian youth to improve their employability and help them earn sustainable livelihoods aligned with industry requirements.

eligibility.otherCriteria: School/college dropouts and previously unemployed youth are given priority

financial: Free skill training courses (short-term, 3 to 6 months)
Monetary reward of Rs. 8,000 average upon skill certification
Placement assistance after training
nonFinancial: Industry-relevant skill certification recognized by NSDC
Soft skills and digital literacy training
RPL (Recognition of Prior Learning) certification for existing workers

howToApply: Step 1: Visit the official PMKVY website or call the helpline
Step 2: Find a nearby PMKVY Training Centre (TC) from the skill india portal
Step 3: Register at the training centre with required documents
Step 4: Complete the skill training program (3 to 6 months)
Step 5: Appear for assessment; receive certification and monetary reward

schemes.name: PM Internship Scheme (PMIS)
schemes.description: A landmark scheme from Budget 2024 providing 1 crore youth with 12-month internships in top 500 Indian companies, offering hands-on industry experience and a monthly allowance.

eligibility.residency: Indian citizen; unemployed and not in full-time education
eligibility.otherCriteria: ITI, polytechnic diploma, BA/BSc/BCom graduates; priority to candidates with lower employability metrics

financial: Rs. 5,000/month allowance (government contribution)
Rs. 6,000 one-time assistance for incidental expenses
Company covers training and administrative costs via CSR funding

howToApply: Step 1: Register on the PM Internship Portal at pminternship.mca.gov.in
Step 2: Fill in educational and personal details
Step 3: Browse and apply to available internship openings in top companies
Step 4: Complete actual skill training as mandated during internship

schemes.name: Startup India Initiative
schemes.ministry: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
schemes.description: India's flagship program for fostering innovation-driven entrepreneurship. It provides recognition, tax benefits, funding support, and regulatory simplification to help young entrepreneurs build and scale startups.

eligibility.ageLimit: 18 years and above (no upper age cap)
eligibility.otherCriteria: Startup must be less than 10 years old; annual turnover should not exceed Rs. 100 crore; should be working on a scalable business model

financial: Tax exemption on profits for 3 years (Section 80-IAC)
Capital gains tax exemption for investors
Access to Fund of Funds (FFS) with Rs. 10,000 crore corpus
Self-certification under 9 labour and environment laws for 3-5 years
nonFinancial: DPIIT recognition certificate
Fast-track patent examination (80% fee concession)
Access to government tenders (relaxed eligibility for startups)
Incubator and mentorship network access
1,97,692+ startups recognized as of 2025

howToApply: Step 1: Register at startupindia.gov.in
Step 2: Submit business plan and company incorporation documents
Step 3: Apply for DPIIT recognition
Step 4: Upon recognition, access tax benefits, funding, and other support
documentsRequired: Certificate of incorporation / LLP / partnership deed
Description of innovative product or service

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana (PMMY)
schemes.description: Provides collateral-free micro-enterprise loans up to Rs. 20 lakh to youth and entrepreneurs for setting up or expanding non-farm income-generating activities.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Non-farm micro/small enterprise; should not be a wilful defaulter

financial: Shishu: Loans up to Rs. 50,000
Kishore: Loans from Rs. 50,001 to Rs. 5 lakh
Tarun: Loans from Rs. 5 lakh to Rs. 10 lakh
Tarun Plus: Loans up to Rs. 20 lakh (for existing Tarun borrowers)
No collateral required

howToApply: Step 1: Visit any bank, MFI, or NBFC that offers Mudra loans
Step 2: Fill the Mudra loan application form
Step 3: Submit business plan and required documents
Step 4: Bank processes and disburses loan upon approval

schemes.name: Post-Matric Scholarship (Central – SC Students)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.description: Provides financial assistance to SC students studying beyond Class 10 (post-matric level) in recognized institutions across India to enable them to complete their education.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Scheduled Caste category; family income below Rs. 2.5 lakh/year

financial: Monthly maintenance allowance (varies from Rs. 380 to Rs. 1,200 based on level and hosteller/day scholar status)
Full tuition fee and non-refundable charges reimbursed
Study tour charges, thesis printing charges, book allowance covered

howToApply: Step 1: Register on the National Scholarship Portal at scholarships.gov.in
Step 2: Fill in personal, academic, and bank details
Step 3: Upload documents
Step 4: Institution verifies; state department disburses the scholarship

schemes.name: Central Sector Scheme of Scholarships for College & University Students
schemes.description: Merit-based scholarship for students from low-income families who are above the 80th percentile in Class 12 board exams and pursuing higher education to ensure that financial constraints do not stop them.

eligibility.ageLimit: Freshers in college (first year); renewal available for 5 years
eligibility.otherCriteria: Above 80th percentile in Class 12; family income below Rs. 4.5 lakh/year; regular full-time college course; not availing other scholarships

financial: Rs. 12,000/year for first 3 years of undergraduate course
Rs. 20,000/year for 4th and 5th year (professional courses)

howToApply: Step 1: Apply on National Scholarship Portal at scholarships.gov.in
Step 2: Fill in Class 12 board, income, and college details
Step 3: Upload documents and submit
Step 4: Institute verifies; scholarship credited to bank account

schemes.name: PM-Vidyalakshmi Education Loan Scheme
schemes.description: A centralized education loan portal providing collateral-free loans up to Rs. 10 lakh with government guarantee for students from families not covered by other government scholarship schemes, to pursue higher education in quality institutions.

eligibility.ageLimit: Students enrolled in recognized higher education institutions
eligibility.otherCriteria: Admitted to QHEIs (Quality Higher Education Institutions); family income below Rs. 8 lakh/year preferred; should not be availing other schemes

financial: Loan up to Rs. 10 lakh (collateral-free)
Government provides 75% credit guarantee to banks
3% interest subvention during moratorium for family income below Rs. 8 lakh/year
Loan for tuition fees, hostel, books, and other education expenses

howToApply: Step 1: Visit the PM Vidyalakshmi portal at vidyalakshmi.co.in
Step 2: Register and fill in personal and academic details
Step 3: Apply to eligible banks offering education loans
Step 4: Bank processes and disburses loan

schemes.name: Agnipath / Agniveer Scheme
schemes.description: A short-term military service scheme allowing youth to serve in the Indian Army, Navy, or Air Force for 4 years, gaining skills, discipline, and financial benefits, with 25% being retained as permanent soldiers.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Must meet physical and educational requirements for respective service branch

financial: Monthly salary starting from Rs. 30,000 and going up to Rs. 40,000 in year 4
Seva Nidhi (corpus) of Rs. 11.71 lakh at exit (tax-free)
Risk and hardship allowances
Rs. 48 lakh life insurance coverage during service
25% chance of permanent enrollment as Soldier (Havildar level)
nonFinancial: Certified military skills and discipline
Priority in state government jobs for many states
Certificate and Mark Sheet for skills acquired

howToApply: Step 1: Watch for official recruitment notifications on joinindianarmy.nic.in / joinindiannavy.gov.in / airmenselection.cdac.in
Step 2: Apply online during the recruitment notification period
Step 3: Appear for written exam and physical fitness test
Step 4: Attend medical examination
Step 5: Join training on receiving appointment letter

schemes.name: National Apprenticeship Training Scheme (NATS)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Education / Board of Apprenticeship Training
schemes.description: Provides engineering graduates and diploma holders with 1 to 2 years of practical apprenticeship training in government and private sector organizations, bridging the gap between academic learning and industry requirements.

eligibility.ageLimit: Freshers and students (18 years and above)
eligibility.otherCriteria: Engineering/diploma graduates from AICTE-approved institutions

financial: Monthly stipend paid by employer (Rs. 9,000 for degree apprentice; Rs. 8,000 for diploma)
Additional government contribution under NAPS (Rs. 1,500/month)
nonFinancial: Hands-on industry experience for 1 to 2 years
National Apprenticeship Certificate upon completion
Higher chance of placement after apprenticeship

howToApply: Step 1: Register on the NATS portal at nats.education.gov.in
Step 2: Search for apprenticeship openings by sector/company
Step 3: Apply for relevant opportunities
Step 4: Attend interview with the company and sign apprenticeship agreement

schemes.name: National Youth Corps (NYC) Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports
schemes.description: A scheme to build a disciplined group of youth volunteers who contribute to nation-building activities including community development, disaster relief, literacy, and environmental awareness.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Unemployed youth with at least matriculation qualification

financial: Monthly honorarium of Rs. 5,000 during volunteer service
nonFinancial: Training in national service and community engagement
Certificate of service recognized for government job applications
Exposure to disaster management, environment, and social work

howToApply: Step 1: Visit the nearest Nehru Yuva Kendra (NYK) in your district
Step 2: Fill in the NYC application form
Step 3: Submit documents and attend selection process
Step 4: Selected volunteers are deployed in community service activities

schemes.name: Prime Minister's Employment Generation Programme (PMEGP)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME)
schemes.description: A credit-linked subsidy scheme to generate self-employment opportunities through the establishment of micro-enterprises in the non-farm sector by helping beneficiaries access bank loans with government subsidy.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Minimum 8th standard pass for projects above Rs. 10 lakh in manufacturing; self-help groups and institutions also eligible

financial: 25% subsidy in urban areas (35% in rural areas) for general category
35% subsidy in urban areas (45% in rural areas) for SC/ST/OBC/minorities/women/ex-servicemen/differently abled
Project cost up to Rs. 50 lakh (manufacturing) and Rs. 20 lakh (services)
Balance funded by bank loan (no collateral for loans up to Rs. 10 lakh)

howToApply: Step 1: Apply online at kviconline.gov.in/pmegpeportal
Step 2: Fill in project details and personal information
Step 3: Submit documents to KVIC/KVIB/DIC
Step 4: Interview and project verification by task force committee
Step 5: Bank sanctions loan and government releases subsidy

schemes.name: Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDU-GKY)
schemes.description: Aims to provide wage employment to rural youth from poor families by providing free market-linked skill training with mandatory placement support post-training.

eligibility.ageLimit: 15 to 35 years (45 for women and other vulnerable groups)
eligibility.otherCriteria: From families covered under SECC 2011; priority to SC/ST, minorities, women, and persons with disabilities

financial: Completely free skill training
Placement in formal sector companies with minimum Rs. 6,000/month salary
Post-placement support for career growth
nonFinancial: Free accommodation and meals during training
Soft skills and English communication training
Industry-recognized certification

howToApply: Step 1: Contact the nearest DRDA (District Rural Development Agency) or DDUGKY training centre
Step 2: Register and fill application form
Step 3: Complete the skill training program (3 to 12 months)
Step 4: Attend placement drives organized by training partners

schemes.name: Stand-Up India Scheme
schemes.description: Facilitates bank loans between Rs. 10 lakh to Rs. 1 crore to at least one SC/ST borrower and one woman borrower per bank branch for setting up greenfield enterprises in manufacturing, services, or trading sectors.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Greenfield enterprise (first venture); not defaulter to any bank/financial institution

financial: Composite loan of Rs. 10 lakh to Rs. 1 crore
Loan covers 75% of project cost including term loan and working capital
Repayment period up to 7 years; moratorium up to 18 months

howToApply: Step 1: Apply through the Stand-Up India portal at standupmitra.in
Step 2: Register and fill business plan details
Step 3: Connect with a Loan Facilitation Centre or directly visit any scheduled commercial bank branch
Step 4: Bank processes and disburses loan
documentsRequired: Proof of address of business premises

schemes.name: Punjab Skill Development Mission (PSDM)
schemes.ministry: Department of Technical Education and Industrial Training, Punjab
schemes.description: A state-level skill training mission to equip Punjab's youth with relevant industry skills, improve employability, and connect them to employment and entrepreneurship opportunities.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Priority to unemployed youth, school dropouts, and youth from marginalized communities

financial: Free skill training in various industry-relevant courses
Stipend during training (for eligible candidates)
nonFinancial: Placement assistance after training
Industry certification recognized by employers
Entrepreneurship development support

howToApply: Step 1: Visit the PSDM website or nearest PSDM training centre in Punjab
Step 2: Register online or at the centre
Step 3: Choose from available skill courses
Step 4: Complete training and appear for certification assessment

schemes.name: Aam Aadmi Rozgar Yojana (AARY) – Punjab
schemes.ministry: Department of Employment Generation, Skill Development & Training, Punjab
schemes.description: A Punjab state scheme to provide employment opportunities to youth through job fairs, skill training, and entrepreneurship support, aiming to connect unemployed graduates with industry and government jobs.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Unemployed graduates and diploma holders registered in Punjab employment exchange

nonFinancial: Access to job fairs organized by Punjab government
Free skill upgrading courses
Entrepreneurship development workshops and mentoring
Connection to MSME and industry partners for placement

howToApply: Step 1: Register on the Punjab employment portal at pgrkam.com
Step 2: Create profile with educational and skill details
Step 3: Browse job listings and apply to job fairs
Step 4: Attend job fairs and interviews organized by AARY

schemes.name: Mukhya Mantri Sehat Bima Yojana (MMSBY) – Punjab
schemes.description: Punjab's flagship health insurance scheme under Ayushman Bharat providing cashless medical treatment up to Rs. 10 lakh per family per year to 100% of Punjab's population in empanelled hospitals.

eligibility.otherCriteria: All Punjab residents are covered (100% population coverage as of 2025)

financial: Cashless health insurance up to Rs. 10 lakh per family per year
Covers 1,949+ medical procedures including surgery, hospitalization, and critical illness
No premium to be paid by beneficiary

howToApply: Step 1: Enroll at the nearest Aam Aadmi Clinic or government hospital in Punjab
Step 2: Register with Aadhaar card for MMSBY health card
Step 3: Use the health card for cashless treatment at empanelled hospitals
Step 4: No pre-authorization required for most treatments
documentsRequired: Ration card or any address proof

schemes.name: NSP – Merit-cum-Means Scholarship for Minorities
schemes.description: Provides financial assistance to students from six notified minority communities (Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, Parsi) pursuing professional and technical courses to boost their career prospects.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Minority community (Muslim/Christian/Sikh/Buddhist/Jain/Parsi); minimum 50% marks in previous year; family income below Rs. 2.5 lakh/year; admitted in recognized professional or technical course

financial: Course fee up to Rs. 20,000 per year
Maintenance allowance of Rs. 10,000 per year

howToApply: Step 1: Apply on National Scholarship Portal at scholarships.gov.in
Step 2: Select Minority Scholarship and fill details
Step 3: Upload required documents
Step 4: Institute verifies; scholarship is released by Ministry of Minority Affairs

schemes.name: AICTE Pragati Scholarship for Girls (Technical Education)
schemes.description: Provides financial support to girl students from low-income families pursuing technical education (diploma or degree level) in AICTE-approved institutions, to encourage more girls to enter technical fields.

eligibility.ageLimit: First and second year diploma or degree students
eligibility.otherCriteria: Family income below Rs. 8 lakh/year; only one girl child scholarship per family per year; admitted to AICTE-approved institution; not availing other central government scholarship

financial: Rs. 50,000 per year (tuition fee or actual, whichever is less)
Covers up to 4 years for degree and 3 years for diploma

howToApply: Step 1: Apply through the AICTE scholarship portal at aicte-india.org
Step 2: Fill in personal, academic, and bank details
Step 3: Upload documents
Step 4: Institution verifies; AICTE releases scholarship annually

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana – Urban (PMAY-U)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
schemes.description: Aims to provide affordable housing to all urban households by 2026, with a special focus on Economically Weaker Sections (EWS), Low Income Groups (LIG), and Middle Income Groups (MIG) through interest subsidies and direct housing construction.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Should not own a pucca house anywhere in India; EWS (income up to Rs. 3 lakh), LIG (Rs. 3 to 6 lakh), MIG-I (Rs. 6 to 12 lakh), MIG-II (Rs. 12 to 18 lakh)

financial: EWS/LIG: Interest subsidy of 6.5% on home loan up to Rs. 6 lakh (for 20 years)
MIG-I: 4% subsidy on loan up to Rs. 9 lakh
MIG-II: 3% subsidy on loan up to Rs. 12 lakh
Direct benefit transfer for beneficiary-led construction

howToApply: Step 1: Apply through pmaymis.gov.in or visit the nearest Urban Local Body (ULB) or Common Service Centre
Step 2: Fill in personal, income, and property details
Step 3: Submit documents for verification
Step 4: After approval, interest subsidy is credited upfront to reduce home loan EMI
documentsRequired: Aadhaar card of applicant and spouse
Affidavit that no member owns a pucca house

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana – Gramin (PMAY-G)
schemes.description: A rural housing scheme providing financial assistance to BPL households in rural areas to construct pucca houses with basic amenities like toilets, electricity, and LPG connections.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Houseless or living in kutcha/dilapidated house; SECC 2011 identified; excludes those with government job or income tax payment history

financial: Rs. 1,20,000 assistance in plains (Rs. 1,30,000 in hilly/difficult areas)
Additional Rs. 12,000 for toilet construction under Swachh Bharat Mission
90 days of MGNREGS wage support for house construction

howToApply: Step 1: Contact Gram Panchayat or Block Development Office
Step 2: Check if your name is in the SECC 2011 permanent waitlist
Step 3: District officials verify and include in the beneficiary list
Step 4: Funds are transferred in instalments based on construction progress

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY)
schemes.description: World's largest financial inclusion initiative providing universal access to banking services with zero balance accounts, free accidental insurance, overdraft facility, and access to pension and insurance schemes.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Unbanked individuals; existing account holders can upgrade to PMJDY

financial: Zero balance savings bank account
RuPay debit card with Rs. 2 lakh accidental death insurance
Overdraft facility up to Rs. 10,000 (for those active for 6 months)
Life insurance cover of Rs. 30,000
Access to micro-credit, pension, and insurance products

howToApply: Step 1: Visit any bank branch or Business Correspondent (BC) nearby
Step 2: Fill the PMJDY account opening form
Step 3: Submit documents
Step 4: Receive passbook and RuPay debit card
documentsRequired: OR: Voter ID, Driving License, or NREGA Job Card

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
schemes.description: A government-backed term life insurance scheme providing Rs. 2 lakh coverage at a nominal annual premium, designed for adults in the working age group from 18 to 50 years.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Must have a bank account with auto-debit facility

financial: Life insurance cover of Rs. 2 lakh on death (any cause)
Annual premium: Only Rs. 436/year (auto-debited from bank account)

howToApply: Step 1: Visit your bank branch or apply through net banking/mobile banking
Step 2: Submit consent for auto-debit of premium
Step 3: Policy gets activated from June 1 of the enrolment year

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY)
schemes.description: A very low-cost accidental insurance scheme providing Rs. 2 lakh cover for accidental death and full disability, and Rs. 1 lakh for partial disability, at just Rs. 20 per year.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Must have a bank account

financial: Rs. 2 lakh on accidental death or permanent total disability
Rs. 1 lakh on permanent partial disability
Annual premium: Only Rs. 20/year

howToApply: Step 1: Visit your bank or apply via net banking/mobile banking
Step 2: Fill enrolment form for PMSBY
Step 3: Premium auto-debited annually from bank account

schemes.name: Ayushman Bharat – PM Jan Arogya Yojana (PM-JAY)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
schemes.description: World's largest government-funded health insurance scheme providing Rs. 5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary hospitalization care to over 10 crore poor and vulnerable families.

eligibility.otherCriteria: SECC 2011 identified families; includes rural poor, unorganized sector workers, urban slum residents; recently extended to senior citizens above 70 years

financial: Cashless and paperless health insurance of Rs. 5 lakh per family per year
Covers 1,949+ medical and surgical packages
Pre and post-hospitalization expenses covered
No cap on family size or age

howToApply: Step 1: Check eligibility at pmjay.gov.in or call the helpline
Step 2: Visit the nearest empanelled hospital or Ayushman Bharat Mitra
Step 3: Get Ayushman Bharat Golden Card (e-card) made with Aadhaar
Step 4: Use the card for cashless treatment at any empanelled hospital
documentsRequired: Ration card or SECC inclusion proof

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas
schemes.description: Provides free LPG connections to women from BPL and marginalized households to replace polluting cooking fuels like firewood and cow dung cakes, improving health and dignity for rural women.

eligibility.otherCriteria: BPL / SECC-identified households; SC/ST families; residents of forest areas; PM Awas Yojana beneficiaries; PMJDY account holders; Tea garden workers; river island residents

financial: Free LPG connection (14.2 kg cylinder with regulator and hose)
EMI option for first refill and stove (recovered from subsidy)
Continued LPG subsidy via PAHAL scheme

howToApply: Step 1: Visit the nearest LPG distributor or Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana camp
Step 2: Fill the KYC form for new LPG connection
Step 3: Submit BPL certificate or SECC details
Step 4: Receive free gas connection with cylinder, regulator, and hose
documentsRequired: BPL card or SECC inclusion proof

schemes.name: MGNREGS – Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme
schemes.description: India's largest employment guarantee program providing 100 days of guaranteed wage employment per year to every rural household whose adult members volunteer to do unskilled manual work.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Any rural adult willing to do unskilled manual work; household registration with Gram Panchayat required

financial: 100 days of guaranteed wage employment per year
Daily wage rate varies by state (Rs. 220 to Rs. 357/day approximately)
Unemployment allowance if work not provided within 15 days
Payment through bank/post office accounts within 15 days of work
nonFinancial: Crèche facility at worksite (if 5+ children under 6 years)
First aid facility at worksite
Shade and drinking water at worksite

howToApply: Step 1: Register at the nearest Gram Panchayat with family details
Step 2: Receive MGNREGS Job Card
Step 3: Submit written application for work at Gram Panchayat
Step 4: Work allocation within 15 days; payment made within 15 days of work completion
documentsRequired: Aadhaar card of all adult members

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemes.description: Provides comprehensive crop insurance coverage to farmers against natural calamities, pests, and diseases, ensuring financial stability to farmers in case of crop failure.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Both loanee and non-loanee farmers cultivating notified crops in notified areas; sharecroppers and tenant farmers also eligible

financial: Full sum insured for total crop loss due to non-preventable risks
Premium for farmers: Only 2% for Kharif, 1.5% for Rabi, 5% for horticulture crops
Balance premium paid by Central and State Government
Coverage for pre-sowing, post-harvest losses, and localized calamities

howToApply: Step 1: Contact nearest bank, Common Service Centre, or insurance company agent
Step 2: Fill the crop insurance application before the cut-off date
Step 3: Pay the applicable premium
Step 4: Claim is settled based on crop cutting experiments and satellite data
documentsRequired: Land ownership records or rental agreement

schemes.name: PM Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemes.description: Provides direct income support of Rs. 6,000 per year to all eligible farmer families to supplement their financial needs and help cover cultivation costs.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Small and marginal farmers; must have arable land in their name; excludes institutional land holders, government employees, income tax payers, professionals (doctors, engineers, etc.)

financial: Rs. 6,000/year transferred in 3 equal instalments of Rs. 2,000 every 4 months
Direct credit to farmer's Aadhaar-linked bank account

howToApply: Step 1: Visit pmkisan.gov.in or the nearest Common Service Centre
Step 2: Register as a new farmer with land and Aadhaar details
Step 3: Submit land records for verification
Step 4: After Patwari verification, name is added to the beneficiary list and payments begin

schemes.name: Deen Dayal Antyodaya Yojana – National Urban Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NULM)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
schemes.description: A scheme to reduce urban poverty by enabling urban poor households to access gainful employment and self-employment through skill development, Self-Help Group formation, and credit linkage.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Urban poor, homeless, rag pickers, street vendors, construction workers; SHG membership preferred

financial: Interest subsidy on loans up to Rs. 2 lakh for individual micro-enterprises
Up to Rs. 10 lakh loan for group enterprises
Revolving Fund for SHGs: Rs. 10,000 per SHG
Shelter facilities for urban homeless
nonFinancial: Free skill training in industry-relevant trades
Placement assistance after training
Support for street vendors to get identity cards and credit

howToApply: Step 1: Contact the nearest Urban Local Body (ULB) or City Livelihood Centre
Step 2: Join a Self-Help Group (SHG) or apply individually for skill training
Step 3: Apply for credit linkage through the ULB
Step 4: Receive training, skill certification, and loan assistance

schemes.name: PMEGP – Entrepreneurship for Adults
schemes.description: For adults in the 25-45 age group seeking to set up new micro enterprises in non-farm sectors with government margin money subsidy to reduce the financial burden of starting a business.

eligibility.ageLimit: 18 years and above (adults in 25-45 bracket specifically eligible)
eligibility.otherCriteria: New enterprise only (not existing businesses); minimum 8th class pass for manufacturing projects above Rs. 10 lakh

financial: Subsidy of 25% (general urban) to 45% (special category rural) of project cost
Project cost up to Rs. 50 lakh (manufacturing) and Rs. 20 lakh (services)
Bank loan for remaining project cost at subsidized interest rate

howToApply: Step 1: Apply at kviconline.gov.in/pmegpeportal
Step 2: Submit project report and documents to KVIC/KVIB/DIC
Step 3: Interview by task force
Step 4: Bank loan sanctioned; government subsidy credited to loan account

schemes.name: Punjab Ghar Ghar Rozgar (PGRKAM) – Job Portal
schemes.description: Punjab's dedicated employment portal connecting job seekers with employers through a state-run digital platform, job fairs, skill training, and career counseling for both public and private sector jobs.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Unemployed or underemployed job seekers; graduates, diploma holders, ITI-trained workers

nonFinancial: Free job portal profile with access to thousands of job listings
Participation in state-organized job fairs
Free career counseling at district employment bureaus
Apprenticeship and internship connections

howToApply: Step 1: Register at pgrkam.com
Step 2: Create your profile with education and skill details
Step 3: Browse job listings or register for upcoming job fairs
Step 4: Apply to relevant jobs or attend placement camps

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri SVANidhi Scheme (PM Street Vendor's AtmaNirbhar Nidhi)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
schemes.description: Provides collateral-free working capital micro-loans to street vendors affected by COVID-19 lockdowns, helping them resume their livelihoods and transition to formal financial systems.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Vendor with Certificate of Vending or Letter of Recommendation from ULB; vendors surveyed in the vendor survey

financial: Initial working capital loan of Rs. 10,000
Enhanced loan of Rs. 20,000 on timely repayment
Further enhanced loan of Rs. 50,000 on continued timely repayment
7% annual interest subsidy on prompt repayment
Digital transactions rewarded with cashback up to Rs. 1,200/year

howToApply: Step 1: Apply through the PM SVANidhi portal at pmsvanidhi.mohua.gov.in
Step 2: Visit the nearest Urban Local Body (ULB) with documents
Step 3: ULB verifies vendor status and issues Letter of Recommendation
Step 4: Apply to a lending institution (bank, MFI) for the loan

schemes.name: Sukh Samman Nidhi Scheme (Punjab) – Women Financial Support
schemes.ministry: Department of Social Security and Women & Child Development, Punjab
schemes.description: A Punjab state scheme providing monthly financial support of Rs. 1,000 to women above 18 years from low-income families to enhance their financial independence and meet daily needs.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Annual family income below Rs. 3 lakh; not a government employee or income tax payer

financial: Rs. 1,000 per month directly credited to bank account

howToApply: Step 1: Apply online at sswcd.punjab.gov.in or visit the nearest Sewa Kendra / DC office
Step 2: Fill the Sukh Samman Nidhi application form
Step 3: Submit income proof and Aadhaar details
Step 4: After verification, monthly payment begins via DBT

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Rozgar Protsahan Yojana (PMRPY)
schemes.description: Encourages employers to hire more workers by having the government pay the entire employer's contribution towards EPF and EPS for new employees earning below Rs. 15,000/month, reducing the cost of hiring.

eligibility.ageLimit: New employees of any age below Rs. 15,000 monthly salary
eligibility.otherCriteria: Employees registered with EPFO for the first time; EPFO-registered employers

financial: Full employer's EPF contribution (12% of wages) paid by government for 3 years
Reduces cost of formal employment for employers by 12%
Workers get EPFO registration and social security benefits

howToApply: Step 1: Employer must be registered with EPFO
Step 2: Hire new employees with salary below Rs. 15,000/month
Step 3: Register new employees on EPFO portal
Step 4: Government automatically pays employer contribution for 3 years

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Vikas Bharat Rozgar Yojana (ELI – Employment Linked Incentive)
schemes.description: A landmark employment scheme announced in Budget 2024 and approved in July 2025 with Rs. 2 lakh crore allocation to create formal jobs for 4.1 crore youth through direct salary support for first-time employees and incentives for manufacturing employers.

eligibility.ageLimit: First-time employees in formal sector (18-35 years primarily)
eligibility.otherCriteria: First-time EPFO registrant for Scheme A; manufacturing sector for Scheme B; employers adding 50+ new employees for Scheme C

financial: Scheme A: One-month salary up to Rs. 15,000 for first-time employees (in 3 instalments)
Scheme B: Up to Rs. 3,000/month reimbursement to manufacturing sector employers for 2 years
Scheme C: Employer gets reimbursement for new hires above 1,000 additional employees
Mandatory financial literacy course for Scheme A second instalment

howToApply: Step 1: First-time employees register on the EPFO portal when joining formal employment
Step 2: Employer registers the new employee in EPFO
Step 3: Government directly transfers Scheme A benefit to employee's Aadhaar-linked account
Step 4: Manufacturing employers apply through labor ministry portal for Scheme B and C reimbursements
